text.title: FinOps Framework — INFORM / OPTIMIZE / OPERATE Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: FinOps work MUST progress through INFORM → OPTIMIZE → OPERATE in order; skipping phases produces noisy decisions and unsustainable savings.
rule.rationale: Optimizing without visibility leads to wrong decisions; operating without optimization preserves waste.

rule.statement: A named FinOps practitioner / lead MUST own the program with explicit RACI for each cost decision class.
rule.rationale: Anonymous programs fade; named ownership creates feedback loops with stakeholders.

rule.statement: FinOps council MUST meet on a fixed cadence (≥monthly) with Eng, Finance, Business representation. Async-only fails for cross-functional coordination.
rule.rationale: Cloud cost decisions cross functional boundaries; sync forums force alignment.

rule.statement: Each phase MUST publish phase-specific KPIs with baselines and deltas. INFORM: coverage, dashboard adoption. OPTIMIZE: waste rate, RI/SP coverage. OPERATE: budget variance, automation coverage.
rule.rationale: Without phase-specific KPIs the program drifts into theatre; KPIs anchor what 'progress' means.

rule.statement: Each phase MUST define an exit gate (e.g. INFORM exits when tag coverage ≥80% and dashboards exist). Programs MAY iterate on phases but MUST not jump gates.
rule.rationale: Phase gates prevent the program from claiming OPERATE-level maturity while INFORM artefacts are absent.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) cloud spend <$100k/yr, OR (b) no cross-functional sponsorship, OR (c) program is in pure cost-cutting mode (single-shot, no sustainability requirement).
rule.rationale: FinOps framework overhead requires scale + sponsorship + sustainability; without them, lighter tools win.
